problem_statement.title: `ansible-galaxy` CLI ignores `validate_certs` settings for custom Galaxy servers
problem_statement.description:
### Summary

While working with private Automation Hub / Galaxy servers that use self-signed or internally signed certificates, the `ansible-galaxy` CLI does not appear to respect the `validate_certs` configuration defined in `ansible.cfg`.

As per the [Galaxy server list configuration options](https://docs.ansible.com/projects/ansible/latest/collections_guide/collections_installing.html#:~:text=Galaxy%20server%20list%20configuration%20options), this should be supported.

This seems to be a known limitation as per this code comment: https://github.com/ansible/ansible/blob/60a9be7f46780d4f46e2618cb9f56fb419b9dee6/lib/ansible/cli/galaxy.py#L81-L83.

### Issue Type

Bug Report

### Component Name

ansible-galaxy

### Ansible Version

```console
$ ansible --version
ansible [core 2.20.3]
  config file = /home/nchakrab/Downloads/ansible.cfg
  configured module search path = ['/home/nchakrab/.ansible/plugins/modules', '/usr/share/ansible/plugins/modules']
  ansible python module location = /home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/lib/python3.13/site-packages/ansible
  ansible collection location = /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections:/usr/share/ansible/collections
  executable location = /home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/bin/ansible
  python version = 3.13.2 (main, Feb  9 2025, 12:43:55) [GCC 14.2.1 20250110 (Red Hat 14.2.1-7)] (/home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/bin/python)
  jinja version = 3.1.6
  pyyaml version = 6.0.2 (with libyaml v0.2.5)
```

### Configuration

```console
$ ansible-config dump --only-changed -t all
CONFIG_FILE() = /home/nchakrab/test-ee/ansible.cfg
EDITOR(env: EDITOR) = /usr/bin/nano
GALAXY_SERVER_LIST(/home/nchakrab/test-ee/ansible.cfg) = ['private_automation_hub_published']
PAGER(env: PAGER) = less

GALAXY_SERVERS:

private_automation_hub_published
================================
token(/home/nchakrab/test-ee/ansible.cfg) = superSecretToken
url(/home/nchakrab/test-ee/ansible.cfg) = https://my_pah.org/api/galaxy/content/published/
validate_certs(/home/nchakrab/test-ee/ansible.cfg) = False
```

### OS / Environment

NAME="Fedora Linux"
VERSION="43 (Workstation Edition)"

### Steps to Reproduce

<!--- Paste example playbooks or commands between quotes below -->
1.  Create an ansible.cfg with the following contents:
```
[galaxy]
server_list=private_automation_hub_published

[galaxy_server.private_automation_hub_published]
url=https://<AAP GW FQDN OR IP>/api/galaxy/content/published/
validate_certs=false
token=superSecretToken
```

2. Assuming that your PAH published repository has a collection named `my_org.my_ns`, run the following:
```
ansible-galaxy collection install my_ns.my_col
```

### Expected Results

Cert verification for the configured Galaxy server is skipped and the collection is installed successfully.

### Actual Results

```console
$ ansible-galaxy collection install my_ns.my_col --force -vvvv
ansible-galaxy [core 2.18.14]
  config file = /home/nchakrab/Downloads/ansible.cfg
  configured module search path = ['/home/nchakrab/.ansible/plugins/modules', '/usr/share/ansible/plugins/modules']
  ansible python module location = /home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/lib/python3.13/site-packages/ansible
  ansible collection location = /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections:/usr/share/ansible/collections
  executable location = /home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/bin/ansible-galaxy
  python version = 3.13.2 (main, Feb  9 2025, 12:43:55) [GCC 14.2.1 20250110 (Red Hat 14.2.1-7)] (/home/nchakrab/.pyenv/versions/core/bin/python)
  jinja version = 3.1.6
  libyaml = True
Using /home/nchakrab/Downloads/ansible.cfg as config file
Starting galaxy collection install process
Loading collection community.general from /usr/share/ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/general
Found installed collection community.general:11.4.2 at '/usr/share/ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/general'
Loading collection ansible.posix from /usr/share/ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/posix
Found installed collection ansible.posix:2.1.0 at '/usr/share/ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/posix'
Loading collection ansible.netcommon from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/netcommon
Found installed collection ansible.netcommon:8.4.0 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/netcommon'
Loading collection ansible.utils from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/utils
Found installed collection ansible.utils:6.0.1 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/utils'
Loading collection community.vmware from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/vmware
[WARNING]: Collection community.vmware does not support Ansible version 2.18.14
Found installed collection community.vmware:* at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/vmware'
Loading collection ansible.mcp_builder from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/mcp_builder
Found installed collection ansible.mcp_builder:1.0.0-rc2 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/ansible/mcp_builder'
Loading collection cisco.ios from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/cisco/ios
Found installed collection cisco.ios:11.3.0 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/cisco/ios'
Loading collection cisco.nxos from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/cisco/nxos
Found installed collection cisco.nxos:11.1.100 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/cisco/nxos'
Loading collection community.FIXME from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/FIXME
Found installed collection community.FIXME:0.1.0 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/community/FIXME'
Loading collection my_ns.my_col from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/my_ns/my_col
Found installed collection my_ns.my_col:1.0.2 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/my_ns/my_col'
Loading collection vmware.vmware from /home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/vmware/vmware
Found installed collection vmware.vmware:2.7.0 at '/home/nchakrab/.ansible/collections/ansible_collections/vmware/vmware'
Process install dependency map
Initial connection to galaxy_server: https://54.208.88.26/api/galaxy/content/published/
Found API version 'v3, pulp-v3' with Galaxy server private_automation_hub_published (https://54.208.88.26/api/galaxy/content/published/)
Opened /home/nchakrab/.ansible/galaxy_token
Calling Galaxy at https://54.208.88.26/api/galaxy/content/published/v3/collections/my_ns/my_col/
Starting collection install process
Server https://54.208.88.26/api/galaxy/content/published/ has not signed my_ns.my_col:1.0.2
Fetching a collection tarball for 'my_ns.my_col:1.0.2' from Ansible Galaxy
Downloading https://54.208.88.26/api/galaxy/v3/plugin/ansible/content/published/collections/artifacts/my_ns-my_col-1.0.2.tar.gz to /home/nchakrab/.ansible/tmp/ansible-local-39429bic764ug/tmpblvrll4m/my_ns-my_col-1.0.2-jlhg3x9e
```

### Code of Conduct

- [x] I agree to follow the Ansible Code of Conduct

evaluation_criteria.gold_patch:
diff --git a/changelogs/fragments/ansible-galaxy-validate_certs-server-config.yml b/changelogs/fragments/ansible-galaxy-validate_certs-server-config.yml
new file mode 100644
index 00000000000000..e162464859cab0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/changelogs/fragments/ansible-galaxy-validate_certs-server-config.yml
@@ -0,0 +1,4 @@
+bugfixes:
+  - >-
+    ansible-galaxy collection - Fix using the server configuration for ``validate_certs``
+    when downloading collections. (https://github.com/ansible/ansible/issues/86694)
diff --git a/lib/ansible/cli/galaxy.py b/lib/ansible/cli/galaxy.py
index 9ce8cf7cf4be01..8ef7b7fb3cbb74 100755
--- a/lib/ansible/cli/galaxy.py
+++ b/lib/ansible/cli/galaxy.py
@@ -78,8 +78,10 @@ def method_wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
         if 'artifacts_manager' in kwargs:
             return wrapped_method(*args, **kwargs)
 
-        # FIXME: use validate_certs context from Galaxy servers when downloading collections
-        # .get used here for when this is used in a non-CLI context
+        # configures validate_certs for type 'url' only
+        # type 'galaxy' inherits and overrides resolved_validate_certs
+        # type 'git' recalculates resolved_validate_certs
+        # NOTE: .get used here for when this is used in a non-CLI context
         artifacts_manager_kwargs = {'validate_certs': context.CLIARGS.get('resolved_validate_certs', True)}
 
         keyring = context.CLIARGS.get('keyring', None)
diff --git a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py
index 958dae035b00d9..c7240bb25a849c 100644
--- a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py
+++ b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/concrete_artifact_manager.py
@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@
 from ansible import context
 from ansible.errors import AnsibleError
 from ansible.galaxy import get_collections_galaxy_meta_info
-from ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error
+from ansible.galaxy.api import should_retry_error, CollectionVersionMetadata, GalaxyAPI
 from ansible.galaxy.dependency_resolution.dataclasses import _GALAXY_YAML
 from ansible.galaxy.user_agent import user_agent
 from ansible.module_utils.common.text.converters import to_bytes, to_native, to_text
@@ -71,8 +71,7 @@ def __init__(self, b_working_directory, validate_certs=True, keyring=None, timeo
         self._artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, bytes]
         self._galaxy_artifact_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, bytes]
         self._artifact_meta_cache = {}  # type: dict[bytes, dict[str, str | list[str] | dict[str, str] | None | t.Type[Sentinel]]]
-        self._galaxy_collection_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate | Requirement, tuple[str, str, GalaxyToken]]
-        self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache = {}  # type: dict[Candidate, tuple[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]
+        self._galaxy_collection_cache: dict[Candidate, tuple[CollectionVersionMetadata, GalaxyAPI]] = {}
         self._b_working_directory = b_working_directory  # type: bytes
         self._supplemental_signature_cache = {}  # type: dict[str, str]
         self._keyring = keyring  # type: str
@@ -107,15 +106,12 @@ def require_build_metadata(self, value):
 
     def get_galaxy_artifact_source_info(self, collection):
         # type: (Candidate) -> dict[str, t.Union[str, list[dict[str, str]]]]
-        server = collection.src.api_server
 
         try:
-            download_url = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection][0]
-            signatures_url, signatures = self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection]
+            metadata, server = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]
         except KeyError as key_err:
             raise RuntimeError(
-                'The is no known source for {coll!s}'.
-                format(coll=collection),
+                f"There is no known source for {collection!s}"
             ) from key_err
 
         return {
@@ -123,14 +119,13 @@ def get_galaxy_artifact_source_info(self, collection):
             "namespace": collection.namespace,
             "name": collection.name,
             "version": collection.ver,
-            "server": server,
-            "version_url": signatures_url,
-            "download_url": download_url,
-            "signatures": signatures,
+            "server": server.api_server,
+            "version_url": metadata.signatures_url,
+            "download_url": metadata.download_url,
+            "signatures": metadata.signatures,
         }
 
-    def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):
-        # type: (t.Union[Candidate, Requirement]) -> bytes
+    def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection: Candidate) -> bytes:
         """Given a Galaxy-stored collection, return a cached path.
 
         If it's not yet on disk, this method downloads the artifact first.
@@ -141,11 +136,10 @@ def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):
             pass
 
         try:
-            url, sha256_hash, token = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]
+            metadata, api = self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection]
         except KeyError as key_err:
             raise RuntimeError(
-                'There is no known source for {coll!s}'.
-                format(coll=collection),
+                f'There is no known source for {collection!s}'
             ) from key_err
 
         display.vvvv(
@@ -155,39 +149,27 @@ def get_galaxy_artifact_path(self, collection):
 
         try:
             b_artifact_path = _download_file(
-                url,
+                metadata.download_url,
                 self._b_working_directory,
-                expected_hash=sha256_hash,
-                validate_certs=self._validate_certs,
-                token=token,
+                expected_hash=metadata.artifact_sha256,
+                validate_certs=api.validate_certs,
+                token=api.token,
             )  # type: bytes
         except URLError as err:
             raise AnsibleError(
                 'Failed to download collection tar '
-                "from '{coll_src!s}': {download_err!s}".
-                format(
-                    coll_src=to_native(collection.src),
-                    download_err=to_native(err),
-                ),
+                f"from '{api!s}': {err!s}"
             ) from err
         except Exception as err:
             raise AnsibleError(
                 'Failed to download collection tar '
-                "from '{coll_src!s}' due to the following unforeseen error: "
-                '{download_err!s}'.
-                format(
-                    coll_src=to_native(collection.src),
-                    download_err=to_native(err),
-                ),
+                f"from '{api!s}' due to the following unforeseen error: "
+                f'{err!s}'
             ) from err
         else:
             display.vvv(
-                "Collection '{coll!s}' obtained from "
-                'server {server!s} {url!s}'.format(
-                    coll=collection, server=collection.src or 'Galaxy',
-                    url=collection.src.api_server if collection.src is not None
-                    else '',
-                )
+                f"Collection '{collection!s}' obtained from server {api!s} "
+                f"{api.api_server!s}"
             )
 
         self._galaxy_artifact_cache[collection] = b_artifact_path
@@ -368,15 +350,13 @@ def get_direct_requires_ansible(self, collection: Candidate) -> str | None:
         # NOTE: Using None as a sentinel since it's not a valid value otherwise.
         return runtime.get("requires_ansible")
 
-    def save_collection_source(self, collection, url, sha256_hash, token, signatures_url, signatures):
-        # type: (Candidate, str, str, GalaxyToken, str, list[dict[str, str]]) -> None
-        """Store collection URL, SHA256 hash and Galaxy API token.
+    def save_collection_source(self, collection: Candidate, metadata: CollectionVersionMetadata, api: GalaxyAPI) -> None:
+        """Store collection version metadata and origin.
 
         This is a hook that is supposed to be called before attempting to
         download Galaxy-based collections with ``get_galaxy_artifact_path()``.
         """
-        self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection] = url, sha256_hash, token
-        self._galaxy_collection_origin_cache[collection] = signatures_url, signatures
+        self._galaxy_collection_cache[collection] = (metadata, api)
 
     @classmethod
     @contextmanager
diff --git a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/galaxy_api_proxy.py b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/galaxy_api_proxy.py
index 18c6e6304e1e46..2effd275c8a430 100644
--- a/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/galaxy_api_proxy.py
+++ b/lib/ansible/galaxy/collection/galaxy_api_proxy.py
@@ -146,11 +146,8 @@ def get_collection_version_metadata(self, collection_candidate: Candidate) -> Co
             else:
                 self._concrete_art_mgr.save_collection_source(
                     collection_candidate,
-                    version_metadata.download_url,
-                    version_metadata.artifact_sha256,
-                    api.token,
-                    version_metadata.signatures_url,
-                    version_metadata.signatures,
+                    version_metadata,
+                    api
                 )
                 return version_metadata
evaluation_criteria.success_command: TODO: Inject repo-specific success command (e.g., 'npm map', 'go test ./...')
